title: CHILDREN IN THE MUSAAJID
excerpt: CHILDREN IN THE MUSAAJID
body:
CHILDREN IN THE MUSAAJID

Nabi (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said: “Keep your children and insane people away from your Musaajid.”

The Musaajid are the Houses of Allaah Ta’ala and they deserve the utmost respect and reverence. We have been created for the worship of Allah Ta`ala, and the highest form of worship is Salaat. The Musaajid are the structures built specifically to execute this highest form of Ibaadat. The sanctity of the Musaajid cannot be over-emphasised. 

Not only is purity and cleanliness imperative in the Musaajid, they are also abodes of peace and tranquillity.

Little children pose a grave threat to both – the purity and tranquillity – of the Musaajid. It is for this reason that Nabi g expressly forbade their entry.

When the Shariah prohibits an Ibaadat such as thikr and even the Qur’aan Shareef to be recited loudly in the Musaajid, what then can be said of the raucous behaviour created by delinquent children!

A Hadith explains that when people speak (worldly or futile speech) in a Musjid, the angels curse such a person. Do parents who bring their little children into the Musjid desire that the curse of Allah Ta’ala and His angels fall on their children? In fact, the parents are liable for the disturbances created by their children.

It is stated in Al Ashbaah wan Nazaa`ir that it is HARAAM to bring such children into a Musjid who have no perception of ritual cleanliness and who are themselves impure. If they are not impure and have some perception of ritual cleanliness, then too, it is Makrooh to bring them to the Musjid. [In the vocabulary of the Fuqahaa, ‘Makrooh’ denotes Tahreem. This means that to regularly bring small children (younger than the age of 7, who may even be well-behaved) to the Musjid is Makrooh Tahreemi – in other words – HARAAM).

When a child reaches the age of 7 and he is properly trained (at home) regarding cleanliness and he understands and respects the sanctity of the Musjid, he may be brought into the Musjid, otherwise not!

Children (who qualify to be brought into the Musjid) should also be taught to stand in a separate saff behind the adults. Children who stand amongst the adults, break the saff, thereby causing a deficiency in the Salaat of the adults.

Besides what has been explained above regarding small children, today there is a greater problem than  the small children, and that is the unruly behaviours of baaligh (mature) ‘children’ in the Musjid. They show no respect for the Salaat, the Musjid and the musallis. Many teenagers have absolutely no perception of the sanctity of the Musjid, and their parents display no concern for the misbehaviour of their baaligh ‘children’. Despite them being adults in terms of the Shariah, their behaviour at times is worse than the nabaaligh (immature) children. It is the incumbent obligation of parents to instil in their children the significance and importance of the House of Allah Ta’ala.

 

May Allah Ta`ala grant us all the proper understanding of the Deen.

 .entry-content

title: O Muslims, you have been afflicted with Wahan
excerpt: O MUSLIMS! YOU HAVE BEEN AFFLICTED WITH WAHAN
body:
O MUSLIMS! YOU HAVE BEEN AFFLICTED WITH WAHAN

Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said: “The people will soon summon one another to attack you as people when eating invite others to share their dish.” Someone asked: “Will that be because of our small numbers at that time?” He (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) replied: “No, you will be numerous at that time: but you will be scum and rubbish like that carried down by a torrent, and Allah will remove fear of you from the breasts of your enemy and cast wahan into your hearts.” Someone asked: “What is wahan.” Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) replied: “Love of the world and dislike of death.”

Precisely as our Master (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) had predicted, the ummah today has reached this pitiful, despicable state. The ‘ulama’ in our midst are the catalysts that have perpetuated this sad state upon the Ummah. Our ‘ulama’ that head the numerous Jamiats, Daarul Ulooms, Musaajid, Tabligh jamaats, etc. have proven their spinelessness for all to see.

No sooner had the authorities ‘declared’ their unjust, oppressive decrees, our ‘muslim leaders’ needed no coaxing or time to consider their options, or think to consult Allah Ta`ala’s Kitaab and the Ahaadith for directives, rather they jumped on the panic wagon, and instinctively licked the boots of their masters.

If we consider carefully, our condition has sunk to a level even lower than what the Hadith outlines. Not only has the fear for the Ummah left the hearts of the disbelievers, but fear for them has overwhelmed the weak hearts of ‘muslims’.

Love for the dunya – love for name and fame – and an intoxicated craving for acceptance and recognition from the disbelieving authorities has rendered many ‘muslim’ leaders totally blind to the Truth and left them running around like headless chickens. Added to this, their fear of death has deprived them of any Imaan they had left in their bosoms. They forget who the Controller of the COVID-19 virus is.

As of today, TWO days AFTER the government issued their oppressive decrees, the Christian ZCC church has still not made a decision on whether they will proceed with their annual easter ‘pilgrimage’. Whether they go ahead or not, is not the issue presently. The point is that even these disbelievers, have the guts to hold off and consider their options before making hasty decisions. If the government decree was ‘absolute’ this delay of theirs would warrant legal action. Nothing has happened. On the other hand, our Musaajid have been humiliated by these cretins masquerading in religious garb. Shame on them!!

The fear of ‘muslims’ today for everything and everyone, besides Allah Rabbul Izzat, has delegated them to a level worse than scum and filth! The Blessed Words of Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) have come to pass, and the present Ummah are the ones being described in the Hadith mentioned above.

 

May Allah Ta`ala have mercy on us, and may He raise this Ummah to the glorious mantle it deserves.                                                                                                  [22 Rajab 1441 / 17th March 2020]

 .entry-content

title: NIKE – THE BRAND AND EMBLEM OF PURE KUFR
excerpt: –     NIKE –
body:
–     NIKE –

THE BRAND AND EMBLEM OF PURE KUFR

Allah Ta`ala declares in the Qur`aan Majeed, “Do not ascribe partners to Allah. Without doubt, Shirk is the worst injustice.”

 [Surah Luqmaan, Aayat 13]

The clothing brand named “Nike” has a pure kufr and shirk (polytheistic) connotation.

“In Greek mythology, Nike is the Winged Goddess of Victory. The logo is derived from goddess’ wing, ‘swoosh’, which symbolises the sound of speed, movement, power and motivation.” [www.economictimes.indiatimes.com] 

“The sports equipment company Nike, Inc. is named after the Greek goddess Nike.” [Wikipedia]

A MUSLIM’S REACTION TO A CHRISTIAN CROSS

That it is Haraam for any Muslim to wear clothing with a Christian Cross, is known even to the Muslim with the weakest Imaan and no sane Muslim would even ask for proof to justify this. 

If any person enters the Musjid with a Christian Cross emblazoned across his clothing, even the weakest of Muslims would take umbrage, and most would even insist the transgressor removes such offensive clothing. In fact, no Muslim would even wear shoes that have the Cross advertised on it!

What would the reaction of a Musalli in a Musjid be if the person standing in front of him has a large Christian Cross emblazoned across the back of his jacket or jersey? Not only would the person be asked to remove the clothing item, if he refuses, he would in all probability be forcefully ejected from the Musjid. After all, which Muslim wants to perform Salaat with a Christian Cross right in front of him?

THIS IS EVEN WORSE THAN WEARING A CROSS!

Now, even worse than this Christian Cross is the “Nike” name and emblem. Both represent something directly associated to ascribing partners to Allah Rabbul Izzat. Shirk (polytheism) is by far worse than Christianity. Amongst the worst of people to walk the earth are the Mushrikeen and the greatest sin that can be committed by humankind is that of Shirk!

It is Haraam for any Muslim to buy, sell or wear any item of clothing that has the name “Nike” emblazoned on it or even if it simply has the “Swoosh” emblem. Both are representative of polytheism and hence totally haraam.

Wearing such clothing/shoes is active propagation of kuffar and shirk!!!

SALAAT IS NOT ACCEPTED!

If a person performs Salaat with such clothing, the Salaat will not be accepted. It will be obligatory to repeat such Salaat after removing this offensive clothing.

What can be said of the gall of that person who stands right in the House and in the Presence of Allah Rabbul Izzat, shamelessly and brazenly advertising a Greek goddess? (Nauthubillah!!!)

Any income earned through the sales of such clothing will likewise be Haraam.

DESTROY IT

If a Muslim owns any such item of clothing etc. that has this name or emblem of polytheism, it is Waajib to destroy it. It will not be permissible to give such items away to even non-Muslims!

DESECRATING THE BEAUTIFUL NAME OF ALLAH

We have not even taken into consideration the highly offensive insult this wretched Company cast upon our Creator, by once manufacturing a shoe (takkie) with the Great Name of Allah Ta`ala embossed at the bottom of the sole! This act alone should shake any true Muslim who even has the slightest spark of Imaan to rebel against such blasphemers. Be that as it may, they are Kuffaar and, like shaitaan, this is their purpose in life.

WE APPEAL TO THE IMAANI SENSE OF EVERY MUSLIM TO TOTALLY DISTANCE THEMSELVES FROM THE ISLAMICALLY OFFENSIVE “NIKE” BRAND!

May Allah Ta`ala grant us all the Taufeeq and guidance to recognise the Haqq and may He save us from the evils of Shaitaan and the nafs.

 .entry-content

title: ETHANOL: The bane of the inebriated Scholars for Dollars!
excerpt: ETHANOL
body:
ETHANOL

The bane of the inebriated Scholars for Dollars!

عَنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ، قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏ “‏ إِنَّ مِنَ الْحِنْطَةِ خَمْرًا وَمِنَ الشَّعِيرِ خَمْرًا وَمِنَ التَّمْرِ خَمْرًا وَمِنَ الزَّبِيبِ خَمْرًا وَمِنَ الْعَسَلِ خَمْرًا ‏”‏

Rasulullah (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said: “Indeed khamar (liquor) is made from wheat, khamar is made from barley, khamar is made from dates, khamar is made from raisins and khamar is made from honey.” [TIRMIDHI SHAREEF]

عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ ـ رضى الله عنهما ـ قَامَ عُمَرُ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ فَقَالَ أَمَّا بَعْدُ نَزَلَ تَحْرِيمُ الْخَمْرِ وَهْىَ مِنْ خَمْسَةٍ الْعِنَبِ وَالتَّمْرِ وَالْعَسَلِ وَالْحِنْطَةِ وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالْخَمْرُ مَا خَامَرَ الْعَقْلَ‏.

Hadhrat Ibn Umar (Radhiallahu anhuma) reports that (his father) Umar (Radhiallahu anhu) stood on the pulpit and after praising Allah Ta`ala and sending salutations upon Nabi (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said, “When the Aayat which made khamar haraam was revealed, it (khamar at the time) was produced from five things – grapes, dates, honey, wheat and barley. khamar is that which covers (intoxicates) the brain.”

[BUKHARI SHAREEF]

The issue of the total prohibition of intoxicants such as alcohol is something known to every believer – learned or otherwise. There has been of late a sty-full of Scholars for Dollars (we cannot bear to address them as Ulama or even molvis) that have classified this Haraam as Halaal. This very treacherous act places their Imaan on a precipice of certain ruin. Their ignorance and love for monetary gain have led them to destroy the Imaan of the Muslim Ummah. They perfectly fit the description given to them by Nabi (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) of being the “worst of creation, under the canopy of the sky”.

CHANGING THE NAME

These cretins are fast inviting the Wrath and Punishment of Allah Rabbul Izzat by their wanton disregard of the Deen and Shariah. Their love for earning pennies in lieu of trashy ‘halaal-certificates’, and their yearning for public support by condoning haraam consumption of alcohol, will ultimately lead the Ummah to certain ruin.

عَنْ أَبِي مَالِكٍ الْأَشْعَرِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَيَشْرَبَنَّ نَاسٌ مِنْ أُمَّتِي الْخَمْرَ يُسَمُّونَهَا بِغَيْرِ اسْمِهَا يُعْزَفُ عَلَى رُءُوسِهِمْ بِالْمَعَازِفِ وَالْمُغَنِّيَاتِ يَخْسِفُ اللَّهُ بِهِمْ الْأَرْضَ وَيَجْعَلُ مِنْهُمْ الْقِرَدَةَ وَالْخَنَازِيرَ

Rasulullah (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said, “Soon people from my Ummah will consume khamar calling it by other names, musical instruments will be played for them and singing girls will perform for them. Allah will sink them into the earth and He will make some of them into monkeys and pigs.” [SUNAN IBN MAJAH – Classified Saheeh]

ETHANOL

The prohibition and Hurmat of Ethanol is as clear as daylight. Only those who are obviously dense in the mind are obstinate enough to ignore the obvious facts.

Let us dissect this issue for ease of understanding.

What is Ethanol?

“Ethanol (also called ethyl alcohol, grain alcohol, drinking alcohol, or simply alcohol) is a chemical compound, a simple alcohol with the chemical formula C2H6O. Ethanol is a volatile, flammable, colourless liquid with a slight characteristic odour. It is a psychoactive substance and is the principal type of alcohol found in alcoholic drinks.” [Wikipedia]

“Ethanol is produced from biomass mostly via a fermentation process using glucose derived from sugars (sugar cane, sugar beet and molasses), starch (corn, wheat, grains) or cellulose (forest products) as raw materials.” http://biofuelsassociation.com.au/biofuels/ethanol/how-is-ethanol-made/

Ethanol is chiefly made from grain and wheat. The fact that this is clearly classified as ‘Khamar’ (alcohol) is apparent from the hadith cited at the beginning of this article. This fact alone renders Ethanol Haraam!

Although commercial Ethanol used as an ingredient is chiefly made from corn, grain, etc. the resultant intoxicant and alcohol in wine is also Ethanol!

Those touched by the intoxication of what they consume may stupidly argue that not all Ethanol is made from grain or wheat. This argument is as thin as the intellect they profess to demonstrate. Firstly, there is no way of knowing for certain the Ethanol used commercially is not from wheat or grain. Secondly, even if it is made from some other source, the very fact that it is an intoxicant – an extremely strong one at that – renders it haraam.

THE STRONGEST ARGUMENT OF THE FUQAHA

عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم قَالَ :‏‏ كُلُّ مُسْكِرٍ خَمْرٌ وَكُلُّ مُسْكِرٍ حَرَامٌ

Hadhrat Ibn Umar (Radhiallahu anhuma) reports that Rasulullah (Sallallaahu alaihi wasallam) said, “Every intoxicant is khamar (alcohol) and every intoxicant is Haraam.” [SAHEEH MUSLIM]

This Hadith appears throughout the Kutub of Hadith. Furthermore, it has been reported:

عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ـ صلى الله عليه وسلم ـ ‏ “‏ كُلُّ مُسْكِرٍ حَرَامٌ وَمَا أَسْكَرَ كَثِيرُهُ فَقَلِيلُهُ حَرَامٌ ‏”‏

Abdullah Ibn Umar (Radhiallahu anhuma) reports that Rasulullah (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said, “Every intoxicant is Haraam. That which intoxicates in large quantity, (even) its little quantity is Haraam (even though this little quantity may not intoxicate).” [SUNAN IBN MAJAH]

Based on the above authentic Ahaadith, the Fuqaha have ruled that every intoxicant is Haraam, even though it may not classified under what is termed “Khamar Asli”. The proponents of the permissibility claim that ethanol is not from “Khamar Asli” (alcohol derived from grapes or dates), hence they aver it is not haraam. The Ahaadith cited above prove that every intoxicant is regarded as “Khamar”. It is owing to this technicality that those who claim ethanol is permissible, cannot be classified as kaafir. The Fuqaha are emphatic that any person who regards Khamar as halaal, is kaafir! Although in ‘technical terms’ ethanol may not be classified as Khamar Asli in absolute terms, it is an intoxicant, which does fall under the ruling of Khamar and it is definitely haraam.

RULING OF AHNAAF

It is mentioned in Shaami (one of the most authoritative Kitaabs on Hanafi Fiqh): “Indeed every intoxicant is Haraam, that is, whatever intoxicates in a large quantity, even its smaller quantity is Haraam. This is the view of (Imaam) Muhammed (Rahmatullahi alaih). It is the Mufta Behe view.” [Shami, vol. 3, page 651]

It is also stated in Shaami, vol. 4, page 38:

وَعِنْدَ مُحَمَّدٍ مَا أَسْكَرَ كَثِيرُهُ فَقَلِيلُهُ حَرَامٌ، وَهُوَ نَجَسٌ أَيْضًا قَالُوا: وَبِقَوْلِ مُحَمَّدٍ نَأْخُذُ

“According to Mohammed, whatever intoxicates in large quantity, even its little quantity is Haraam. And it is Najis also. They (the Fuqaha of Ahnaaf) say, “We accept and take the view of Mohammed.”

That is, the unanimous view of the Hanafi Fuqaha, upon which the Fatwa of practice is decreed, every intoxicant is Haraam, regardless of whether it is classified under ‘Khamar Asli’ or not!

The difference of opinion does not come into play here, because if some Scholars for Dollars aver that they prefer to rule in accordance to those Fuqaha who opine that only Khamar Asli is Haraam ( a choice which is not theirs to make anyway, since the preferred ruling has already been decided), then they would be constrained to accept that one tot or a can of beer, vodka, brandy etc. is Halaal, because these drinks contain Ethanol but are not made from grapes or dates!

It is mentioned clearly in Kutub like Badaai`us Sanaa`i, Hidaya, Shaami, etc. that all intoxicants are Haraam, even though they do not fall under the classification of Khamar Asli. According to all our Fuqaha, other intoxicants (besides those made from grapes or dates) also fall under the banner of Khamar, albeit majaazan (metaphorically). 

In the Kitaab, Badaai`us Sanaa`i, in the chapter discussing drinks (Kitabul Ashribati), intoxicants made from wheat and barley (ethanol) is classified and discussed under intoxicants and Khamar.

IT SHOULD BE BORNE IN MIND THAT ACCORDING TO ALL FOUR MATHA-HIB, ALL INTOXICANTS ARE HARAAM. HENCE ETHANOL IS HARAAM FOR ADHERENTS OF ALL FOUR MATHAAHIB.

PRESENCE OF ETHANOL

Brandy: “Brandy is a distilled spirit made from virtually any fermented fruit or starchy vegetable. Distilling concentrates and helps purify the ethanol alcohol created by the fermentation; ethanol is an intoxicant used socially for millennia.” [wwwbbcgoodfood.com]

Vodka: “Vodka is a clear distilled alcoholic beverage that originates from Poland and Russia. It is composed primarily of water and ethanol, but sometimes with traces of impurities and flavourings. Traditionally it is made by distilling the liquid from cereal grains or potatoes that have been fermented, though some modern brands use fruits or sugar as the base.” [Wikipedia]

Beer: “Alcohol, or ethanol, is the intoxicating agent found in beer, wine and liquor.” [www.drugs.com]

Above is just a sample of a few haraam alcoholic drinks. Study properly the excerpt below from Wikipedia that sums up the application of Ethanol:

“An alcoholic drink (or alcoholic beverage) is a drink that contains ethanol, a type of alcohol produced by fermentation of grains, fruits, or other sources of sugar.”

BASED ON THE ABOVE, THERE IS ABSOLUTELY NO DOUBT IN ANY SANE MIND THAT ETHANOL IS ABSOLUTELY AND CERTAINLY HARAAM!!! ETHANOL IS THE ALCOHOL WHICH IS THE INTOXICANT FOUND IN ALL ALCOHOLIC DRINKS, INCLUDING THOSE MADE OF GRAPES AND DATES!

ETHANOL IS NAJIS (IMPURE / FILTHY)

Imaam Mohammed (Rahmatullah alaihi) stated: “That which intoxicates in a large quantity, even its little quantity is Haraam, and it is Najis (impure) also.” [Shaami]

It is also a well-known ruling of Fiqh that alcohol is just as impure as urine, blood, stool, etc. In fact, it is classified as Najaasat Ghalizah. That is, if it soils one’s clothes or body, it is waajib to wash it off, lest one’s Salaat is rendered invalid.

COMMON SENSE

Let us now apply some common sense, after which it will become apparent that the ruling given of permissibility for Ethanol is not only nonsensical, but totally ludicrous. 

We have now established without a shadow of doubt that Ethanol, which according to the kuffaar is, in fact, alcohol per se, is Haraam and impure. This conclusion applies the same to a single drop of Ethanol as opposed to a barrel full of it.

As mentioned previously, if any person wishes to argue in favour of the minority view of some Ahnaaf, that because Ethanol is not necessarily made from grapes or dates, it is not haraam in itself, this argument will be dismissed with the contempt it deserves. In fact, Ethanol is the alcohol that is produced when grapes are fermented to make wine! Based on this, it would even be safe to argue that Ethanol is the Khamar Asli.

Be that as it may, it truly boggles the mind that some Scholars for Dollars stupidly aver that whilst brandy, wine, whisky, beer and other such alcoholic drinks are Haraam because they contain Ethanol, which is the primary intoxicating agent, drinks such as all cold drinks, flavoured waters and drinks, etc. which also contain the very same  Ethanol are permissible!

They claim that the Ethanol used in commercial products are minute in quantity and do not intoxicate, hence permissible. The Ahaadith clearly contradicts their inane claim. Since Ethanol is the chief intoxicant in all alcoholic drinks, it is Haraam there and hence its use in other products, albeit minute in quantity, remains haraam (and najis). Some of these numbskulls stupidly claim that even if you drink copious amounts of cold drink you will not become intoxicated, hence they say it is Halaal. The Ahaadith and Kutub of Fiqh say the opposite! The Shar`i ruling is that since Ethanol intoxicates in brandy, wine, etc. because it exists in larger quantities, it is Haraam even if it is used in small quantities in cold drinks etc. Ethanol is still alcohol, which is still khamar, which remains Haraam and impure, regardless of whether it is found in whisky or Coca Cola or Energade!

Let us use a simple example which even their vacant cranial cavities can comprehend – Let us take for our demonstration two equal-sized glasses.

We will fill the one full with a pure fruit juice and the other half with the same juice. Are the contents of these two glasses halaal and consumable? YES

We will now add just a single drop of urine to the full glass and top up the other glass with urine. Naturally, the contents of the first glass will still look like the juice it contains, and the drop of urine will not be discernible in the least – neither in taste colour or smell. However, the second glass will now look like diluted juice and the contents will most likely smell and taste of urine.

Now, are the contents of these two glasses still pure and consumable? MOST CERTAINLY NOT!

Even a child in basic Maktab class will tell you that when a little napaaki (impurity) falls into a small quantity of water, it will render all that water totally impure.

By what stretch of imagination can the proponents of Ethanol being permissible in soft drinks etc. still rule that it is permissible in small quantities when we have conclusively established that Ethanol is Haraam in any and all quantities and therefore, Ethanol, per se, is also impure?

The Hadith Shareef and Kutub of Fiqh are absolute on the ruling that whatever intoxicates in large quantities (like Ethanol in brandy, whisky, etc) will remain Haraam even if it is consumed in small quantities where it will not intoxicate.

We will repeat our question: If Ethanol is permissible in cold drinks, etc. because it is in small quantities and it does not intoxicate, will it be permissible to consume one tot of whisky or one can of beer, which will not intoxicate also???

COLD DRINKS ETC. ARE NOT ONLY HARAAM, THEY ARE NAJIS (IMPURE)

All such beverages that contain Ethanol, like all cold drinks, fizzy drinks, flavoured waters, energy drinks, essences used in baking, many sweets and confectionaries, amongst them, Flings, etc. are all, WITHOUT A SHRED OF DOUBT, HARAAM AND NAJIS!

Just as a drop of brandy, whisky or beer will contaminate your clothes or body if it touches it, so too will a drop of cold drink!

Many flavourings and almost all essences used in confectionery either contain alcohol or alcohol is used to extract the flavour. All such ingredients are Haraam.

The preposterous argument presented by the inebriated Scholars for Dollars is that the alcohol is ‘evaporated’ in the baking process. We cannot fathom the utter ignorance displayed here. The alcohol gets further permeated into the confectionary rather than ‘evaporated’. The same example of urine can be used – If a teaspoonful of urine is added to a cake and baked, the urine is ‘evaporated’ (according to their stupid argument), will they then consume that cake? The addition of urine made that cake najis, hence Haraam. The very same applies to alcohol. Alcohol, like urine, is haraam to consume and it is also najis.

Why then do they condone Ethanol, but would frown at the mention of urine in its place? The answer is simple, they have learnt to accept the consumption of alcohol and they condone its consumption, thereby literally bringing to fruition the hadith of Nabi (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) that a time will come where people will change the name and designation of alcohol and consume it. Wine and khamar are now disguised as the ingredient ‘Ethanol’, which the Scholars for Dollars of our time have given their (impure) stamp of approval.

May Allah Ta`ala have mercy on this Ummat and guide all the readers to understand the gravity of this sin. Muslims should educate themselves on the ingredients of the foods they consume and become wary of the haraam ingredients.

CONCLUSION

Ø  Ethanol IS ALCOHOL. 

Ø  Ethanol is Haraam. 

Ø  Ethanol is Najis (Impure). 

Ø  Ethanol is what makes wine, brandy, whisky, liquors, beer and all such alcoholic beverages intoxicants. 

 

¬  It is that very same Ethanol that they add to ALL cold drinks, flavoured waters and other ‘fruit’ and ‘energy’ drinks, etc.

¬  It is that very same Ethanol they add to Flings.

¬  It is that very same Ethanol they use in essences and other flavourings used in confectionery and sweets, etc.

CONSIDERING THE FACT THAT NABI (SALLALLAHU ALAIHI WASALLAM), HIS SAHABAH (RADHIALLAHU ANHUM) AND THE FUQAHA HAVE DECREED ETHANOL (AND ALL INTOXICANTS) AS HARAAM AND NAJIS, 

ARE YOU GOING TO CONTINUE CONSUMING THIS HARAAM ETHANOL?

 

 

 .entry-content

title: ACTS OF VIRTUE ON THE 10th OF MUHARRAM
excerpt: Acts of Virtue for the Tenth of
body:
Acts of Virtue for the Tenth of

MUHARRAM

Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said:

“The most virtuous fasting after the month of Ramadhaan is Allah’s month of Muharram.”

Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) was asked about observing the fast on the tenth day of Muharram, and he replied, “It is an expiation for the sins of the preceding year.”

We understand clearly from the Ahaadith Shareef that besides being a Sunnah to fast in Muharram, this is a greatly exhorted practice in our Deen.

It is mentioned in another Hadith where Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) instructed us to add at least one day to the fast of the 10th of Muharram, that is, we should fast on the 9th and 10th or the 10th and 11th of Muharram. If one fasts on all three days (i.e. 9th, 10th and 11th) then it will be most meritorious. The fast of Muharram was ordained in the Shariah before the fasts of Ramadhaan became obligatory. After the fasting of Ramadhaan became Fardh, the fasting of Muharram became optional. This fact highlights the importance of fasting in this month, specifically during these days.

SPENDING ON ONE’S FAMILY

وعن ابن مسعود، قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم “من وسع على عياله في النفقة يوم عاشوراء وسع الله عليه سائر سنته”. قال سفيان: إنا قد جربناه فوجدناه كذلك

Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said:

“He who expands on his family’s sustenance on the day of Ashura, Allah will expand on him throughout the year.”

The word used in this hadith is “nafaqah” (sustenance), which refers to basic expenses. Many of our Akaabireen have limited the expense (as mentioned in the Hadith) on this day to food. 

A commentary of this hadith is also stated in Mishkaat Shareef, where Hadhrat Sufyaan Thauri (rahmatullahi alaih) states: “We have indeed experienced this and found it likewise.” In commenting on this Hadith, it is mentioned in a Sharah (Commentary) that the word “family” in the Hadith refers to one’s immediate household, upon whom one’s expenditure is waajib. This excludes all other family members and friends. Therefore, it is not permissible, rather it is a Bid`ah, to invite others for a meal especially on the Day of Ashura. 

It is further explained in this Sharah that the word, “likewise” of Hadhrat Sufyaan Thauri (rahmatullahi alaih) refers to “expansion in food”. This further entrenches the point that the Hadith clearly refers to spending on food on one’s family and not other extra expenses. It should also be noted that this Hadith applies especially to those who live on basics throughout the year. Such people should make an extra effort to provide a nicer meal to their family on this Day – not doubtful take-out foods, but a pure home-cooked meal. As for the wealthy, they already serve the best they can afford throughout the year. They should divert their funds to feed the poor. Their reward lies in this. Wasting money on doubtful and Haraam restaurant and take-away meals on Aashura is a token of ingratitude to Allah Rabbul Izzat for the bounties He has bestowed us.

GIFTS ON AASHURA

The innovation of giving gifts to family and friends on the Day of Aashura is not only haraam, it is completely unfounded. It is a misinterpretation of the Hadith. Whilst the giving of gifts is a laudable act that warrants great reward, which is further multiplied when giving to one’s family, however, to specifically give gifts on this day is a Bid`ah. It is basically in emulation of the kuffaar-style ‘Mother’s Day’, ‘Father’s Day’, ‘Valentine’s Day’, etc. Showing one’s affection towards family members by showering them with gifts is not limited to any particular day in Islam. This can be done throughout the year! Muslims should abstain from such un-Islamic practices. Giving of gifts on the Day of Ashura and inviting all and sundry for meals will not yield any reward; on the contrary it will be sinful! 

BID`AH ‘CELEBRATIONS’ OF MOURNING

Whilst the very concept of having any function of mourning is reprehensible on its own, this evil is compounded by those who hijack the 10th of Muharram as a day to ‘celebrate’ the martyrdom of great personalities and highlight the event of Karbala. These events occurred decades after the virtues of Muharram were extolled by Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam). Such ‘celebrations’ and functions are not only Haraam and reprehensible, they have absolutely nothing to do with the virtues of Muharram!

 

May Allah Ta`ala save us from the evil of the nafs and Shaitaan.

 

 .entry-content

title: BUKHAARI SHAREEF RECITATION? Yet another Bid’ah in the making
excerpt: Allah Ta`ala declares in the Qur’aan Majeed: “On this day have I completed for you your Deen.”
body:
Allah Ta`ala declares in the Qur’aan Majeed: “On this day have I completed for you your Deen.”

It has been reported in Tirmidhi Shareef from Hadhrat Nafi` (rahmatullahi alaih) that a man sneezed in the presence of Ibn Umar (radhiallahu anhu). The man said, “Alhamdulillahi Wassalaamu ala Rasulillah.”

Hadhrat Ibn Umar (radhiallahu anhu) commented, “I too acknowledge the praises of Allah Ta`ala and send salutations to Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam), however this is not what Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) taught us (to say after sneezing). He taught us to say, ‘Alhamdulillah Ala Kulli Haal.’”

Our Deen has been completed and perfected by Allah Rabbul Izzat. Any act of Deen which is deemed an Ibaadat that has not been practiced or advocated by Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) or his Sahaabah or anyone from the Khairul Quroon, is described as an act of Bid`ah. A Bid’ah is simply translated as an ‘innovation’. Any act of worship or Ibaadat that is ‘new’ to our Deen, is a Bid`ah.

Imaam Rabbani said, “The case with all bid`ah is the same – that is, they are accretions to the Sunnah thereby displacing it.”

A message has been circulating inviting people to a “recitation of Bukhaari Shareef” which is to take place at the Azaadville Darul Uloom Musjid. The message claims: “This has been the action of our pious predecessors that when they were afflicted with some difficulty, they would have a recitation of Bukhaari Shareef…”

Whilst we have never heard of such a practice amongst our pious predecessors, it changes nothing if this ever was a practice of some. It remains a Bid`ah.

We do acknowledge the blessings and power of the Ahaadith of Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) and also the barkat of Bukhaari Shareef (and all the other Saheeh Ahaadith Kutub). There may be some (pious predecessors) who have advised that recitation of the entire Kitaab is a means of barkat, but this would apply to individual recitation and never congregational. This bid`ah as advocated by these persons in Azaadville perfectly fits the description of a Bid`ah! When any act of Ibaadat – or simply put, an act which intends to draw the Mercy of Allah Ta`ala, is done by specifying a time and or place for its execution and inviting others to it, which has never been done or taught during the era of Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) or Khairul Quroon, is branded a BID`AH!

The abuse of the Qunoot Naazilah, which has also reached, nay surpassed, the level of Bid`ah, seems to be insufficient for these Ulama.

They now crave other means of accreditation or recognition to show people they “care” for the plight of the Palestinians.

It is indeed sad that Ulama don’t recognise the plethora of advice from the Qur`aan Majeed and the Sunnah of Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) to draw the Mercy and Aid of Allah Rabbul Izzat in difficult times upon the Ummah, that they resort to the darkness of Bid`ah. Aggrandizement and Hubbe Jah are the only motives we see in this sad scenario. 

The Hadith shows simple remedies to situations like we have in Palestine or in fact any difficulties the Ummah faces:

·       Hadhrat Sa`ad (radhiallahu anhu) reports that Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said: “The supplication of Dhun-Nun (Hadhrat Yunus alaihi salaam) when he supplicated, while in the belly of the whale was 

لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ

So indeed, no Muslim supplicates with it for anything, ever, except Allah responds to him.” [Tirmidhi Shareef]

·       Abu Darda reported: Rasulullah (Sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said, “No Muslim servant supplicates for his brother in his absence but that the angel says: And for you the same.”

·      Abdullah ibn Amr (radhiallahu anhu) reported: Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said, “No supplication is quicker to be answered than a supplication on behalf of one absent.”

The above are just a few examples of simple remedies prescribed by Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) that every Muslim can (and should) do in the privacy of their homes for the Palestinians and for the entire Ummah. Just observe the beauty of the Sunnah in that when we make dua for the Palestinians, Allah Ta`ala will grant us the same protection we ask Him for. There is absolutely no Hadith or anything recorded from the life of our beloved Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) or his Sahaabah (radhiallahu anhum) or Tabieen or Tab`e Taabieen (rahmatullahi alaihim ajmaeen) that advocates or even hints at congregating to recite Ahaadith or make any Thikr or Tilaawat as a means of drawing Allah Ta`ala’s Rahmat. The Hadith mentions the blessings of Surah Yaseen (and other Surahs), but nowhere is it advocated to be recited in congregation or unison. Even in the Qur`aan Majeed, Allah Ta`ala informs us to make dua to Him in silence and that it is such duas that He will readily accept.

Today it is one Darul Uloom that gives birth to the accretion and bid`ah of “Buhaari Shareef recitation” and tomorrow we’ll have all the others follow suit. What about those foreign students (of which almost all our Darul Ulooms have a majority of) who will return to their homes and initiate such Bid`aat which they witnessed their Asaatiza peddling? 

Ulama should realise that the only salvation of this Ummah lies in following the Qur`aan Majeed and Sunnah, specifically in matters of Deen. They should be advancing and propagating the practices of the Sunnah, rather than holding to the views of people who came centuries after Khairul Quroon. When the Shariah has all the remedies to whatever ails the Ummah, why then divert to alien practices? As mentioned above by Mujaddid Alfe Thaani – acts of Bid`ah displace the Sunnah.

The incident above where Hadhrat Abdullah Ibn Umar (radhiallahu anhu) acknowledged the virtue of praising Allah Ta`ala and Durood, however its execution and recitation at a time that was not advocated by Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) rendered it an innovation. It displaced a Sunnah! So even the recitation of something virtuous, when done at an inopportune time and in an inopportune manner meant punishment and not reward.

The irony of this entire “Bukhari Shareef recitation” Bid`ah is that the Noble Ahaadith Shareef is being (ab)used to displace and replace the Sunnah. Inna Lillahi wa Inna Ilaihi Rajioon!

May Allah Ta`ala guide this Ummah.

 

“Clear propagation is our ONLY responsibility”

title: USING THE MISWAAK IMMEDIATELY BEFORE COMMENCING SALAAT – DISPELLING A MISCONCEPTION
excerpt: Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said:
body:
Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said:

السواك مطهرة للفم و مرضاة للرب

“The (use of) miswaak is a cleanliness of the mouth and a (means of gaining the) pleasure of the Rabb.”

The use of the miswaak (toothstick) is undoubtedly a great Sunnah of our beloved Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam). There are many Ahaadith that highlight the virtues and benefits of using the miswaak. The use of toothbrush and toothpaste is not a substitute for the miswaak. Amongst the greatest virtues of using the miswaak is that the person (who regularly uses it) will easily be able to recite the Kalimah at the time of death and also the usage of miswaak during wudhu will ensure the Salaat performed with that wudhu to be 70 times more rewarding.

The following are some of the benefits and virtues of using the miswaak:

1. Miswaak strengthens the gums and prevents tooth decay.

2. Miswaak assists in eliminating toothaches and prevents further increase of decay which has already set in.

3. Miswaak creates a fragrance in the mouth.

4. Miswaak is a cure for illness.

5. Miswaak eliminates bad odours and improves the sense of taste.

6. Miswaak sharpens the memory.

7. Miswaak is a cure for headaches.

8. Miswaak creates lustre (noor) on the face of the one who continually uses it.

9. Miswaak causes the teeth to glow.

10. Miswaak strengthens the eyesight.

11. Miswaak assists in digestion.

12. Miswaak clears the voice.

DISPELLING A MISCONCEPTION

There are some persons who use the Miswaak in the Musjid immediately before Salaat commences. This practice is incorrect, as explained in the Fatwa cited below, which appears in Aap ke Masaail aur un ka Hal:

In responding to a question regarding the usage of a miswaak in Musjid before Salaat, the venerable Mufti Saheb answers:

“The Hadith cited (by those who advocate this practice) is as follows: 

لولا أن أشق على أمتي لأمرتهم بالسواك عند كل صلاة

“Had I not feared difficulty upon my Ummat, I would certainly have ordered them to use the miswaak at the time of every Salaat.”

There is a difference in the words amongst the narrators of this Hadith. Some report this Hadith with the words عند كل صلاة(at the time of every Salaat) whilst others report this Hadith with the words عند كل وضوء(at the time of every wudhu) [Saheeh Bukhari, vol. 12, page 259]. That is, the use of the miswaak is encouraged during every wudhu.

In view of the usage of these differing terms, the import of this Hadith according to Imaam Abu Hanifah (rahmatullahi alaih) is that before every Salaat, wudhu has to be made and we are encouraged to use the miswaak at the commencement of every wudhu. The meaning of the instruction to use miswaak for every Salaat is that it is to be used at the time of making wudhu before the Salaat. The objective is not that miswaak must be made before commencing the actual Salaat.

When a person uses the miswaak when he stands up for Salaat, then there is a fear that the gums may bleed, which will result in the wudhu breaking. If the wudhu breaks, then the Salaat will also be rendered broken. This is the reason why Imaam Abu Hanifah (rahmatullahi alaih) used to say that it is Sunnah to use miswaak before the wudhu which is made for Salaat. Miswaak is not to be made before the Salaat itself.

Besides this (explanation above), miswaak is used to achieve cleanliness of the mouth, and this objective can only be acquired if miswaak is used during wudhu, when water is used to gargle, thereby acquiring proper cleanliness in the mouth. When a miswaak is used just before Salaat commences, then it is done without water or gargling, which will not result in proper cleanliness of the mouth, thereby defeating the objective of the miswaak.”

As explained above, the use of a miswaak immediately before Salaat commences, is not part of the Sunnah. One of the chief objectives of using a miswaak is to clean the teeth, gums and mouth itself. When a (dry) miswaak is used, then besides there being a possibility of the gums bleeding, the dirt which the miswaak removes cannot be expelled from the mouth because one is in the Musjid. If any dirt is removed by the use of the miswaak then either it will remain in the mouth or be swallowed – both options defeat the very purpose of using a miswaak.

 

The best use of a miswaak, is when making wudhu. Alternatively, if one needs to make miswaak (without the need of making a wudhu), then one must wet the miswaak with water and rinse the miswaak and the mouth afterwards.

title: WHAT’S GOOD FOR THE GOOSE…
excerpt: WHAT IS GOOD FOR THE GOOSE…
body:
WHAT IS GOOD FOR THE GOOSE…

In the wake of the recent so-called “World Ijtima” held in Camperdown, KZN, in South Africa, many Ulama have found their inner voices and consciences of Haqq surface. There is a list of Ulama, and even more are invited to add their names to this gilded list to voice their opposition to Molvi Sa`ad of the Nizaamuddeen faction of the now fragmented Tablighi Jamaat. 

The politics that led to this unfortunate (but inevitable) split in the ranks of the Tablighi Jamaat hierarchy is a topic that really does not require any elucidation. Regardless of what many aver, this split boils down to nothing more than a power struggle. Shaitaan has achieved his objective in sullying the once noble reputation of the work of Tabligh. It appears that the expiry date of the effort initiated by sincere personalities is fast approaching. It is sad to note that this downfall is being orchestrated by the leaders of the Tabligh Jamaat themselves – this includes BOTH factions.

There is presently a witch-hunt being orchestrated by the Raiwind faction – the recently declared enemies of the Nizaamudeen faction. They have embarked on a pursuit to vilify Molvi Sa`ad to such an extent that they behave like “dogs with a bone”.

Let us state very clearly and emphatically that we are NOT sympathetic to either faction. The accusations presently being levelled at Molvi Sa`ad are justified and much of what he is claimed to have said is not just nonsense talk, but some of his statements can even be interpretated as kufr. Based on the statements made by Molvi Sa`ad, he is justifiably labelled as a deviate.

The Organisation Wifaqul Ulama has put its weight behind the Raiwind faction and circulated articles penned by its senior Ulama. A couple of its senior Ulama – by the way, Wifaqul Ulama also appear to be a rudderless and leaderless group, much like the Raiwind faction – have openly voiced their criticism and opposition to Molvi Sa`ad. Again, allow us to reiterate, we do not disagree with what is being said of this Molvi Sa`ad. We have not personally discussed with Molvi Sa`ad to get his perspective on his statements, but when one listens to his talks, it does speak volumes on the man’s ideologies. Suffice to say that he is a misguided miscreant!

When one carefully studies the criticisms levelled against Molvi Sa`ad, many of which are outlined in a Fatwa of the Madrasah in Isipingo and circulated by Wifaqul Ulama, it becomes abundantly clear that most of his views and utterances are common to all members of the Tabligh Jamaat. Long before this split even occurred, we have heard members of the Tabligh Jamaat imply that their “work” is the “only work”. Right to this day, just observe any Jamaat in any Musjid and you will see that they will commence their bayaan whilst Musallis are still engaged in Salaat in the Musjid! They constantly crow that “ijtimai amal” (collective action) is superior to “Infiraadi amal” (Individual action). So, to them there is greater merit in sitting in the Kitaab reading than completing Nafl Salaat! 

The above cited Fatwa mentions that Molvi Sa`ad claims (inter alia) that “Dawah and Taleem is confined to the Musjid”. Right to this day, the Raiwind faction followers doggedly insist on conducting their Kitaab reading  inside the Musjid (thereby disturbing Musallis performing Sunan and Nawaafil) instead of reading in the sections adjoining the Musaajid. They insist and persist in having their Taleem inside the Musjid. And then there is their mantra of “making the Musaajid Aabaad” (keeping the Musaajid occupied), but where were all these people during the Covid lockdown???

Like this we can draw similitudes to almost every single accusation being levelled at Molvi Sa’ad in this Fatwa to what the Raiwind faction practice to this day.

As for his statements that are akin to kufr, even these are nothing new in Jamaat circles and bayaans. We speak first-hand and our claim is not based on hearsay! All through the years, statements similar to those made today by Molvi Sa`ad have been uttered by Tablighis. In fact, if the Nizaamuddeen faction installs spies to listen to the talks at Raiwind they would also probably come up with many ‘objections’ like those made against Molvi Sa`ad.

And then we can draw the very clear distinction of the likeness between the leaders of both factions. Just as Molvi Sa`ad has shown himself to be a deviate, the very same can be said of Tariq Jameel who is a ‘senior’ on the Raiwind Shura. Tariq Jameel is an open faasiq and mudhil (one who misleads). He is an open Shiah-sympathiser. He is guilty of making halaal what Allah Ta`ala has decreed Haraam.

There is also the case of Molvi Ahmed Laat – another ‘senior’ of the Raiwand faction, who is also an open faasiq and faajir. He appears on videos on YouTube.

…SHOULD BE GOOD FOR THE GANDER

The issue we wish to highlight here is the following:

In this Fatwa, the Mufti Saheb states: “Therefore, these Ulama and trustees stopping these jamaats from speaking in their masjids conforms to the law of Shari’ah.  Just as we will not allow other deviated sects (e.g. Shi’ahs, Qadiyaanis and Salafis etc.) to give bayaans in our masaajid, as it is possible that during the bayaan, they may utter something against Deen, similarly we will not allow the jamaats aligned with Ml Sa’d to deliver bayaans in our masaajid, for the same reason and based on the same fear.”

In this statement, the Mufti Saheb has condemned many a sincere Jamaat workers from “doing the work”. Because of the statements of one man, they condemn the entire following. They liken people (with whom they had always worked together with and sat side by side) to kaafir Shiahs and Qadiyanis!

We can safely claim that around 90% (if not more) of the followers of the Nizaamuddeen faction have never heard bayaans of or even set eyes on Molvi Sa`ad. They almost certainly would not bring faith in his statements of kufr if they were made aware of them. To dump them all into the same basket as Shiahs and Qadianis is indeed lamentable and very shortsighted.

We find it strange that the brothers in the Nizaamuddeen camp are likened to Shiahs and Qadianis. A Shiah is a Shiah because he subscribes to the kufr ideologies of Shiasm. The same can be said of Qadianis that they believe in and follow the corrupt ideologies of Qadianism.

The people linked to Nizaamuddeen are not regarded as Tablighis because they believe in or follow the ideologies of Sa’ad. They are Tablighis because they follow the very same dictates and system that the Raiwind fellows follow! It is illogical that they all be damned and discarded into the denizen of hellfire with Sa’ad.

It seems that the disease of extremism and fanaticism has gripped the minds of Sa’ad’s detractors just as it has him. But that is their modus operandi so they should apply this same logic to the followers and members of other deviant organisations.

NEVERTHELESS

If this Mufti Saheb, Wifaqul Ulama and the many ulama who have backed the opposition to Molvi Sa`ad stand by this contention of theirs – that all the followers of Nizaamuddeen faction are deviates, and that they should not be allowed to conduct programs in our Musaajid, then in all fairness, this very same ruling must be levelled at the Raiwind faction as well. The rot at the heads of both factions is equally putrid!

Why are these Ulama being so selective? Why single out Molvi Sa`ad and Nizaamuddeen, when Raiwind and (some of) its leaders are guilty of similar wrongs. 

Extremism is a sickness of many Tablighis. Aayaat of the Qur`aan Majeed and also Ahaadith are interpreted incorrectly by all of them. The wrongs of the individuals connected to the Jamaat work are many. However, the ‘work’ itself is honourable and praiseworthy. By formulating such Fataawa and by the senior Ulama making such a hue and cry and condemning the entire faction of Nizaamuddeen, they are shooting the Jamaat Effort in the foot. They are almost literally “cutting their noses to spite their faces”. If they are not careful in their tirade against Molvi Sa`ad and condemning the entire faction, they will end up destroying the image of the entire Tabligh Jamaat effort.

THE GANDERS ARE MANY…

Let us now turn our attention to the other obvious ‘elephants in that room’.

Let us once again repeat – We are in agreement with much of the criticism levelled against Molvi Sa`ad by his detractors. We are extremely pleased that so many Ulama have ‘stood up’ for the Haq. Many of whom have all along been ‘dumb shayaateen’ and strangely silent when local deviant molvis and organisations have trampled the Haqq and propagated Baatil and kufr for many years. Many senior Ulama have also now openly spoken and even mentioned the name of Molvi Sa`ad in their condemnation of him (and unfortunately of the entire Nizaamuddeen faction).

We now call upon all these Ulama and Organisations to denounce, condemn and declare as deviates and corrupt, the following organisations AND THEIR MEMBERS AND FOLLOWERS:

JAMIATUL ULAMA SOUTH AFRICA (sic), UUCSA (based in Mpumalanga), SANHA, RADIO ISLAM (sic), MJC, DARUL IHSAAN, etc.

These organisations, or at least its leaders, are guilty of greater kufr than Molvi Sa`ad. Some of Molvi Sa`ad’s statements are heinous and evil, but they pale in comparison to the great evil and act of kufr of aligning with the kuffaar to keep the Musaajid closed?  We are all aware of the events during the Covid era, when the above miscreants (all friends to one another) introduced themselves as “friends of the Court” and stood alongside the non-Muslim government to keep the Houses of Allah Ta`ala closed!

The open transgressions being perpetrated by all of the above deviant miscreants on a daily basis are too numerous to mention. They engage in Haraam acts and perpetrate kufr in full view of the masses. Not only do they change haraam into halaal, but they also encourage others to follow them.

EXPOSING THE DUST OF ANONYMITY

The misdemeanors of Molvi Sa’ad appear to have some attraction to certain Ulama. It brings out the Haq in them, Alhamdulillâh. But they should not stop there.

Following the Camperdown ijtima, a group styling itself “Kashful Ghubaar” seemed to have appeared out of thin air critiquing Sa’ad’s bayaans.

The identity of this group lurks behind a cloud of dust. Their articles don’t even have an email address. Be that as it may, it’s almost as though they exist just to find fault in the talks of Molvi Sa’ad. The point of Ikhlaas from amongst the 6 points of the Tablighi Jamaat appears to be lost to them. Nevertheless, we eagerly await their exposition of the deviant Zanaadeeq, the likes of Bham, Menk, the 2 Sulaimans, Navlakhi, Zubair Bhayat, Tariq Jameel and the myriad of other ‘shuyookh’. We urge them to expel the clouds of dust that enshroud the kufr and dhalaal of these rascals.

WE URGE THE MANY ULAMA WHO HAVE, ALHAMDULILLAH, RECOGNISED THE LIGHT OF HAQQ, TO STAND UP, JUST AS THEY HAVE AGAINST MOLVI SA`AD, AND CONDEMN THE ABOVE DEVIANT ORGANISATIONS AND ISSUE FATWAS PROHIBITING THE MEMBERS OF THE ABOVE ORGANISATIONS FROM EVER GIVING TALKS OR HOLDING PROGRAMS IN OUR MUSAAJID!

And then, in all fairness, these Ulama who have stood against Molvi Sa`ad, must also declare ALL the followers, well-wishers and members of the above organisations as deviates and they must all be lumped together with Molvi Sa`ad, his followers, the Shiahs and Qadianis! No member or employee of the Jamiatul Ulama SA (based in Fordsburg and headed by the deviate Bham) or the other deviant organisations must be allowed to hold any program in any Musjid or even perform Imaamat.

After all, WHAT IS GOOD FOR THE GOOSE, MUST ALSO BE GOOD FOR THE GANDER!

 

O ULAMA! DO NOT LOSE YOUR VOICE OF HAQQ NOW THAT YOU HAVE FOUND IT FOR MOLVI SA`AD. CONDEMN THE BAATIL THAT LINGERS AMONGST YOUR RANKS!

 .entry-content

title: ALLAH RABBUL IZZAT BECOMES ANGRY AND HIS ARSH SHAKES WHEN A FAASIQ IS PRAISED
excerpt: Hadhrat Anas ibn Malik (radhiallahu anhu) said: “When a faasiq (open sinner) is praised, Allah Ta`ala gets angry and the Arsh (Divine Throne) shudders on account of (Allah Ta`ala’s anger).”
body:
Hadhrat Anas ibn Malik (radhiallahu anhu) said: “When a faasiq (open sinner) is praised, Allah Ta`ala gets angry and the Arsh (Divine Throne) shudders on account of (Allah Ta`ala’s anger).”

An article circulated by Jamiat KZN recently praising the faasiq ex-leader of Hamas (Ismail Haniyeh) speaks volumes of their ignorance – both of current affairs and especially the Shariah. They stupidly add a special dua – Rahimahullah – meant for pious Muslims after the name of the faasiq Haniyeh. This article of theirs, most surely invites the Anger of Allah Ta`ala and contains atrocious and stupid claims. They make the man out to be a humble person. They falsely claim that he lived a “simple life amongst the residents of Gaza”. What atrocious stupid lies. 

Muslims should not allow their Imaan and the Deen to be clouded by emotion. The plight of the Palestinians is dire and every Muslim throughout the globe has sympathy for their plight and together with duas for their safety and Imaan, millions (nay billions) of Rands (and dollars) are donated towards the cause of the Palestinians. The Muslim Community, internationally, daily entreat our Creator to assist the Palestinian Muslims against the cruelty and oppression of the Israelites and Zionists who are doomed for damnation. 

As Muslims we need to accept that whatever events are playing out in Palestine and all over the universe are through Divine Decree. Allah Ta`ala is Most Just and Merciful. There is wisdom in every single action of His. After all, Allah Rabbul Izzat reminds us in His Kalaam that not even the falling of a single leaf occurs without His Knowledge.

THE ADVICE OF HADHRAT UMAR (RADHIALLAHU ANHU)

عن عَبْد اللَّهِ بْن عُتْبَةَ قَالَ قَالَ عُمَر بْن الْخَطَّابِ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ إِنَّ أُنَاسًا كَانُوا يُؤْخَذُونَ بِالْوَحْيِ فِي عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَإِنَّ الْوَحْيَ قَدْ انْقَطَعَ وَإِنَّمَا نَأْخُذُكُمْ الْآنَ بِمَا ظَهَرَ لَنَا مِنْ أَعْمَالِكُمْ فَمَنْ أَظْهَرَ لَنَا خَيْرًا أَمِنَّاهُ وَقَرَّبْنَاهُ وَلَيْسَ إِلَيْنَا مِنْ سَرِيرَتِهِ شَيْءٌ اللَّهُ يُحَاسِبُهُ فِي سَرِيرَتِهِ وَمَنْ أَظْهَرَ لَنَا سُوءًا لَمْ نَأْمَنْهُ وَلَمْ نُصَدِّقْهُ وَإِنْ قَالَ إِنَّ سَرِيرَتَهُ حَسَنَةٌ 

Hadhrat Abdullah bin Utbah reports, “I heard ‘Umar bin Khattab (radhiallahu anhu) saying: ‘In the lifetime of Rasulullah (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) some people were called to account through Wahi. Now Wahi (Revelation) has discontinued and we shall judge you by your apparent acts. Whoever displays to us good, we shall grant him peace and security, and treat him as a near one. We have nothing to do with his intention. Allah will call him to account for that. But whosoever shows evil to us, we shall not grant him security neither shall we believe him, even if he professed that his intention is good.” [Saheeh Bukhari]

This ex-leader of Hamas dressed and looked just like any regular disbeliever. If a Muslim does not have the love for Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) and Islam to even try and imitate the dressing and appearance of our beloved Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam), how can he ever claim to be ‘fighting’ for Allah Ta`ala and Islam? All the slogans these so-called ‘jihadists’ shout are hollow and only meant to attract name, fame and money! True love for Allah Ta`ala lies in emulating the Sunnah of Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam).

Let us study this man dispassionately:

1.     He dressed and outwardly appeared like a non-Muslim. He did not even sport a Sunnah beard!

2.     He died in the land of the greatest enemy of Islam – Iran! He went there in support of a Shiah. He was attending the inauguration of the Shiah president! He was there to honour a kaafir Shiah, and yet the Jamiat KZN honour him. How very, very shortsighted.

3.     His Janazah was performed by a Shiah.

4.     According to numerous reports, he was worth FOUR BILLION DOLLARS at the time of his death.

5.     He lived in Qatar whilst his fellow countrymen were languishing in squalor, starvation and constant bombing.

This man’s death can never be classified as martyrdom. Let us look at this recent fracas in the Middle East. Hamas apparently initiated the hostilities by bombing civilians. Whilst every Jew is accursed, Islam does not teach us to kill non-combatant civilians. If Hamas comprised intelligent Muslims, they could have attacked some military target of Israel. Or even targeted some Israeli dignitary. By attacking these civilians, they must have surely anticipated some retaliation from the Israeli army. Were they ready for this backlash? Apparently not! Did these Hamas people not anticipate that Israel will attack the Palestinian civilian population in retaliation to their brash action?

One can surmise that this Hamas attack was designed to gain sympathy from the international community, especially Muslims, who would then open their hearts and wallets to the ‘Hamas Cause’! This suspicion is backed by the fact that this Hamas ex-leader – who by the way earned his livelihood in this leadership position – was worth FOUR BILLION DOLLARS! How is it that a person who supposedly fought for the Palestinians had such a fortune when his fellow countrymen were dying of starvation! The world had to rally around the Palestinians and send food, whilst the leader of Hamas swam in BILLIONS of dollars! Hamas (also Haniyeh) have been accused over the years of misappropriating and stealing aid meant for the Palestinians. To this day, many people who ostensibly collect funds for Palestine, pocket most (if not all) for themselves. 

If these people truly were fighting a Jihad, they would have faced the Israeli usurpers head-on. Why waste your energies and weapons on civilians, when you can – or should be – attacking the enemy army? All these so-called ‘jihadists’ are good for is un-intelligent guerrilla warfare which ultimately results in mass Muslim civilian casualties. If they were sincere and stood up to the Jewish aggressors, Allah Ta`ala will come to their aid and assist them in vanquishing the accursed Israeli army. True Jihad is spreading the Kalimah of Allah Ta`ala and expanding the borders of Islam. With the wealth these people (like Hamas) usurp from the international community, they can easily muster a powerful Muslim army to stand up against and also defeat the disbelievers.

We make dua that true Mujaahideen, the likes of Salahuddeen Ayubi (rahmatullah alaih), stand up to defend the Muslims of Palestine and expand the borders of Islam. We have the consolation prophesied by Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) of the coming of Hadhrat Imaam Mahdi (alaihi salaam) who will remove Muslims from the debased state we have allowed ourselves to be subjected to.

Muslims should not be so naïve to allow themselves to be blinded by superficial ‘heroism’. Muslims are guided by the Shariah and the Sunnah. A Faasiq is a Faasiq, until he reforms himself. Ismail Haniyeh lived a life of Fisq and died with it. His condition now is only known to Allah Ta`ala. We need to study the situation as we see it and draw rational conclusions. We should not be blinded by pseudos’ and facades. We must take lesson from the words of Hadhrat Umar (radhiallahu anhu) as cited above. We do not know what the bosoms of man conceal. We should only be affected by the zaahir (outer appearances) of people and draw our conclusions from what we can see and by what our Islamic intellects compute.

When Ulama praise fussaaq (plural of faasiq), it sends a wrong message to the Muslim masses. Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) said that an intelligent person is one who suppresses his naffs and base desires and carries out actions that will benefit his Aakhirah. This means that a Muslim should follow the Shariah and the Sunnah. The fact that a man is Palestinian and claims to be fighting the cause of the Palestinians should not emotionally blind true believers. His stupendous wealth amassed on the backs and blood of his fellow countrymen, and the fact that he was fraternising with the enemies of Islam and the Sahaabah (radhiallahu anhum) clearly denote otherwise.

It is as clear as daylight that to people like Ismail Haniyeh, the plight of Palestinians is just a way for them to fill their coffers. It is all about dirty politics. Jihad, Islam, Allah Ta`ala and Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam) are furthest from their minds. True love for Islam and Allah Ta`ala can be found in the practical emulation of the life of Nabi (sallallahu alaihi wasallam).

 

May Allah Ta`ala guide this Ummah.

 .entry-content

title: DUAS TO BE RECITED WHEN MAKING QURBAANI AND AQEEQAH
excerpt: FOR QURBANI RECITE:
body:
FOR QURBANI RECITE:

إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا أَنَا مِنْ الْمُشْرِكِينَ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَبِذَلِكَ أُمِرْتُ وَأَنَا اَوَّلُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ 

بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ اَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ

AQEEQAH DUAS

FOR BOYS:

  اَللّهُمَّ هذِهِ عَقِيْقَةُ  (mention boy’s name)   دَمُهَا بِدَمِهِ وَ لَحْمُها بِلَحْمِهِ وَ عَظْمُهَا بِعَظْمِهِ وَ جِلْدُهَا بِجِلْدِهِ وَ شَعْرُهَا بِشَعْرِهِ

FOR GIRLS:

اَللّهُمَّ هذِهِ عَقِيْقَةُ         (mention girl’s name)    دَمُهَا بِدَمِهَا وَ لَحْمُهَا بِلَحْمِهَا وَ عَظْمُهَا بِعَظْمِهَا وَ جِلْدُهَا بِجِلْدِهَا وَ شَعْرُهَا بِشَعْرِهَا

 THEREAFTER RECITE:

إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا أَنَا مِنْ الْمُشْرِكِينَ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ وَبِذَلِكَ أُمِرْتُ وَأَنَا اَوَّلُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ 

بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ اَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ

If one cannot remember the above duas, then at the very least:

بِاسْمِ اللَّهِ اَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ

MUST be verbally recited before slaughtering, failing which the animal will NOT be Halaal.
